summary: Tested in-app messaging feature end-to-end. Found and fixed 2 critical bugs: (1) Navbar.jsx used `unreadMessages` without destructuring from useWebSocket() - ReferenceError. (2) WebSocketProvider was only wrapping dashboard routes, so /messages page crashed with 'useWebSocket must be used within WebSocketProvider'. Fixed by moving WebSocketProvider to app-level in App.js. After fixes, all 13 backend messaging API tests pass and frontend /messages page works correctly.

integration_issues.issue: FIXED: MessagesPage crashed with 'useWebSocket must be used within WebSocketProvider' because WebSocketProvider was only on crew/contractor dashboard routes
FIXED: Navbar.jsx referenced `unreadMessages` variable without calling useWebSocket() - would cause ReferenceError on all non-dashboard pages

critical_code_review_comments: WebSocketProvider was scoped too narrowly (only crew/contractor dashboards) - any page using useWebSocket (Navbar, MessagesPage) would crash outside dashboards. Fixed by hoisting to app level.
Navbar.jsx had `useWebSocket` imported but not called, leading to undefined variable usage. Fixed by destructuring unreadMessages from useWebSocket() call.
seed_data_creation: admin_chat thread was created for crew1 during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Messaging feature is fully working. Backend APIs 100% pass. Frontend /messages page loads, shows thread list, Navbar dropdown shows Messages link with unread badge. Crew dashboard shows Admin Support button. WebSocketProvider is now at app level (not per-route). Free crew users see upgrade lock on message input (correct).

rca_of_issues.bug: MessagesPage crash: useWebSocket must be used within WebSocketProvider
rca_of_issues.root_cause: App.js only wrapped WebSocketProvider around /crew/dashboard and /contractor/dashboard routes. MessagesPage (/messages route) and Navbar on other pages had no WebSocketProvider ancestor.
rca_of_issues.fix: Moved WebSocketProvider from per-route to wrap the entire BrowserRouter in App.js

rca_of_issues.bug: Navbar unread badge - ReferenceError: unreadMessages is not defined
rca_of_issues.root_cause: Navbar.jsx imported useWebSocket but never called it, yet used `unreadMessages` on lines 79 and 141
rca_of_issues.fix: Added `const { unreadMessages } = useWebSocket();` in Navbar component
